components.name: API Gateway (FastAPI Application)
responsibilities: Expose all REST API routes under /api/v1/ prefix with OpenAPI 3.1 documentation at /docs
Enforce JWT authentication middleware: validate Authorization: Bearer token on all protected routes, extract role from JWT claims (developer | manager)
Issue JWT access tokens (TTL=1h) and refresh tokens (TTL=7d, HttpOnly cookie) via POST /api/v1/auth/login and POST /api/v1/auth/refresh
Rate-limit inbound requests per user (100 req/min per developer, 200 req/min per manager) to prevent quota exhaustion on the LLM explanation provider API
Validate request payloads against Pydantic models derived from OpenAPI schemas before delegating to service modules
Mount and coordinate all service modules: profile ingestion, matching engine, explanation, risk, erasure, feedback, analytics, weight config
Expose GET /api/v1/health endpoint (no auth required) for deployment health-check
Return standardized error envelope: {error_code, message, request_id} on all 4xx and 5xx responses

components.name: Profile Ingestion Service
responsibilities: CRUD operations for DeveloperProfile: POST /api/v1/developers, GET /api/v1/developers/{id}, PUT /api/v1/developers/{id}, DELETE (delegated to GDPR handler)
CRUD operations for ProjectProfile: POST /api/v1/projects, GET /api/v1/projects/{id}, PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}, DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}

Validate all required DeveloperProfile fields on ingest: id, skills (list), experience_years, preferred_stack (list), work_style vector (list[float], len=8), motivation_vector (list[float], len=8), timezone (IANA string), availability_hours (int), career_goals (list[string]), project_history (list)

Validate all required ProjectProfile fields: id, required_skills, team_structure, workload_intensity (float 0.0–1.0), innovation_level (float 0.0–1.0), timezone_overlap_required (string), duration_weeks (int), growth_opportunities (list)
Flag profiles where behavioral vectors (work_style, motivation_vector) are self-reported vs inferred via is_self_reported boolean field
Enqueue embedding generation task to embedding-worker after every profile create or update
Persist and serve WeightConfig via GET/PUT /api/v1/config/weights; validate that |sum(w1..w5) - 1.0| < 0.001 and each wi >= 0.0 before persisting

components.name: Matching Engine
responsibilities: Compute MATCH_SCORE = w1*skill_score + w2*workstyle_score + w3*motivation_score + w4*timezone_score + w5*growth_score for a given (DeveloperProfile, ProjectProfile) pair
Load runtime-configurable weights w1–w5 from WeightConfig table on each match request (no in-process caching to ensure weight changes take effect immediately)
Compute skill_score as Jaccard similarity between developer.skills and project.required_skills weighted by experience_years relevance
Compute workstyle_score as cosine similarity between developer.work_style vector and project team_structure behavioral profile embedding (queried from vector-embedding-store)
Compute motivation_score as cosine similarity between developer.motivation_vector and project.innovation_level + growth_opportunities embedding
Compute timezone_score from overlap between developer.timezone and project.timezone_overlap_required using a time-zone distance matrix
Compute growth_score by matching developer.career_goals against project.growth_opportunities using keyword overlap and ANN embedding similarity
Persist MatchRecord with developer_id, project_id, match_score, component scores, weights_snapshot, explanation (stub or cached), explanation_source, risks, growth_potential, and timestamp
Expose POST /api/v1/matches and GET /api/v1/matches/{match_id} and GET /api/v1/developers/{id}/matches
Return top-K ranked project recommendations for a given developer on GET /api/v1/developers/{id}/matches?top_k=10
Expose POST /api/v1/matches/rescore endpoint to re-rank all or subset of matches on demand (batch re-optimization)

components.name: Claude Explanation Service
responsibilities: Load versioned prompt template from artifacts/prompts/match_explanation_v1.json at service startup; reload on SIGHUP for zero-downtime prompt updates

Produce synchronous deterministic stub explanation in <10ms from match scoring data when cache miss occurs; stub must contain skill-alignment sentence, behavioral-fit sentence, and growth-potential sentence to satisfy AC3

Enqueue asynchronous LLM API call for full explanation after stub is returned; call the configured LLM provider per ADR-004 (Phase-1: Google Gemini gemini-1.5-flash; Claude API claude-3-5-haiku is a supported alternate)
Cache full LLM explanations keyed on SHA-256(developer_profile_hash + project_profile_hash + weights_snapshot_hash); cache stored in relational-db ExplanationCache table with TTL=7 days
Invalidate cache entries on PUT /api/v1/config/weights (weights_snapshot_hash changes)
Implement retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, initial delay 1s, max delay 8s) for LLM API failures
Return explanation_source ENUM field: 'stub_pending', 'claude_cached', 'claude_async' to allow clients and tests to distinguish explanation maturity
Expose GET /api/v1/matches/{match_id}/explanation for clients to poll for the full LLM explanation
Never expose raw work_style or motivation_vector values to the LLM prompt; pass only computed dimension scores and profile metadata

components.name: Risk Prediction Engine
responsibilities: Compute burnout_risk_score (float 0.0–1.0) for a developer: score > 0.6 when AllocationRecord shows >= 48 consecutive weeks at workload_intensity >= 0.8; formula: burnout_risk = min(1.0, (consecutive_high_intensity_weeks / 48) * workload_intensity_mean * (1 - motivation_alignment_factor))

Compute bench_risk_score (float 0.0–1.0): score > 0.7 when developer's current project end_date is within 28 days and no AllocationRecord with start_date in the next 28 days exists; formula: bench_risk = min(1.0, max(0.0, (28 - days_until_end) / 28))
Expose GET /api/v1/developers/{id}/risk returning {developer_id, burnout_risk_score, bench_risk_score, computed_at}
Expose GET /api/v1/teams/{team_id}/risk-summary returning aggregated risk distribution for manager dashboard (no raw vectors, only risk scores and badge states)
Support batch risk refresh via POST /api/v1/risk/refresh to re-compute scores for all developers on schedule

components.name: GDPR Erasure Handler
responsibilities: Orchestrate full cascade deletion for DELETE /api/v1/developers/{id}: execute as a single PostgreSQL transaction covering (1) DeveloperProfile row deletion, (2) all developer_embeddings rows for the developer_id, (3) all MatchRecord rows where developer_id matches, (4) all FeedbackRecord rows where developer_id matches, (5) all AllocationRecord rows, (6) ExplanationCache entries keyed to the developer
Return HTTP 204 on successful erasure; return HTTP 404 if developer_id not found
If transaction fails (e.g., pgvector deletion error), roll back entire transaction and return HTTP 500 with error detail; log failure to audit table for manual remediation
Create an ErasureAuditLog entry on each DELETE request recording: developer_id, requested_at, completed_at, status (completed | failed), initiating_user_id
Expose GET /api/v1/admin/erasure-audit/{developer_id} (manager-role only) to verify erasure completion for compliance reporting
After transaction commit, validate developer is no longer accessible: internal GET by developer_id must return None from DB

components.name: Feedback and Analytics Service
responsibilities: Store FeedbackRecord on POST /api/v1/matches/feedback: persist developer_id, match_id, accepted (boolean), feedback_timestamp; return HTTP 201

Compute rejection_ratio on GET /api/v1/analytics/rejection-rate?developer_id={id}: rejection_ratio = count(accepted=false) / count(*) from FeedbackRecord for developer_id; return {developer_id, rejection_ratio, sample_count, min_sample_floor_met (bool)}

Enforce minimum sample floor: return min_sample_floor_met=false and rejection_ratio=null when sample_count < 10 (per requirements risk mitigation) to avoid surfacing statistically unreliable alerts to managers
Expose GET /api/v1/analytics/team-rejection-rate?team_id={id} for manager dashboard aggregated view
Expose GET /api/v1/analytics/match-stats?developer_id={id} returning total_matches, accepted_count, rejected_count, rejection_ratio (or null if below floor)
Never expose raw behavioral vectors or match component scores in analytics responses; only aggregate rejection statistics

components.name: Embedding Generation Worker
responsibilities: Consume embedding generation tasks enqueued by profile-ingestion-service via FastAPI BackgroundTasks (Phase 1 in-process queue; Phase 2 migrate to Celery + Redis if throughput demands it)

Generate skill embedding: encode developer.skills + developer.preferred_stack + developer.experience_years as text and compute 1536-dim vector via text-embedding model (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small or local sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2)
Generate behavioral embedding: encode developer.work_style vector + developer.motivation_vector + developer.career_goals as structured text and compute 1536-dim vector
Generate project skill embedding: encode project.required_skills + project.team_structure as text
Upsert embeddings into developer_embeddings / project_embeddings pgvector tables
Mark developer profile embedding_status as 'ready' in DeveloperProfile table after successful embedding; mark as 'failed' and enqueue retry on error
Batch re-embed all profiles on embedding model change (triggered via POST /api/v1/admin/reembed)

components.name: Vector Embedding Store (pgvector)
responsibilities: Store skill and behavioral embedding vectors for all developer and project profiles in pgvector-typed columns (VECTOR(1536))

Provide ANN search via HNSW index with cosine distance operator (<=>): SELECT developer_id, embedding <=> $1 AS distance FROM developer_embeddings WHERE embedding_type = 'behavioral' ORDER BY distance LIMIT $2
Support exact DELETE by developer_id or project_id for GDPR cascade within PostgreSQL transaction
Maintain HNSW index parameters: m=16, ef_construction=64 (optimized for 10K–100K vector range with <10ms query latency)
Co-located within the same PostgreSQL instance as relational-db to enable single-transaction GDPR cascade operations
Rebuild HNSW index offline (REINDEX CONCURRENTLY) when embedding model is upgraded to new dimensionality

components.name: Relational Database (PostgreSQL 15)
responsibilities: Persist all structured entities: UserAccount, DeveloperProfile, ProjectProfile, MatchRecord, FeedbackRecord, AllocationRecord, PromptVersion, WeightConfig, ExplanationCache, ErasureAuditLog

Enforce foreign key constraints: MatchRecord.developer_id -> DeveloperProfile.id (ON DELETE CASCADE), MatchRecord.project_id -> ProjectProfile.id (ON DELETE CASCADE), FeedbackRecord.match_id -> MatchRecord.id (ON DELETE CASCADE)
Execute Alembic migrations on startup: manage schema evolution across environments without manual SQL
Serve synthetic seed data via Alembic data migration: >= 50 DeveloperProfile rows and >= 20 ProjectProfile rows with realistic field values for CI and development
Support horizontal read scaling via read replicas for Phase 2 (write to primary, read from replica for GET /developers, GET /projects, GET risk endpoints)
Store WeightConfig as a single-row configuration table with versioning; PUT /api/v1/config/weights performs an upsert with updated_at timestamp
Enable pgvector extension (CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector) in the first Alembic migration

components.name: React Frontend (Developer and Manager Dashboards)
responsibilities: Render Developer Dashboard: display ranked list of recommended project cards for the authenticated developer; each card shows project name, match_score (formatted as percentage), LLM-generated explanation string, risk tags, and growth potential bullets

Render Manager Dashboard: display team composition health panel with per-developer risk badges (bench_risk, burnout_risk) derived from GET /api/v1/developers/{id}/risk; exclude raw behavioral vectors, work_style arrays, and motivation scalar values from all rendered output
Implement JWT auth flow: POST /api/v1/auth/login on form submit, store access_token in memory (not localStorage), store refresh_token in HttpOnly cookie, refresh access token on 401 responses

Call backend REST API endpoints as defined in artifacts/api-contracts.json; handle explanation_source='stub_pending' by displaying a loading indicator and polling GET /api/v1/matches/{match_id}/explanation
Submit match feedback (accept/reject) via POST /api/v1/matches/feedback on developer interaction with recommendation cards
Build with Vite + React 18 + TypeScript; serve static build artifacts from /frontend/dist via FastAPI StaticFiles mount in development or a CDN in production

runtime.build_command: pip install -r requirements.txt && alembic upgrade head && cd frontend && npm install && npm run build

failure_modes.scenario: LLM API timeout or rate-limit error during async explanation generation
failure_modes.mitigation: Explanation service retries with exponential backoff (3 attempts: 1s, 2s, 4s). Match response always returns a synchronous stub explanation within 500ms per ADR-003; the LLM call happens after the response is returned. After all retries exhausted, the stub remains as the final explanation and explanation_source is set to 'stub_permanent'. Alert is logged to ErasureAuditLog-equivalent monitoring table. No user-facing error occurs.

failure_modes.scenario: LLM API rate limit (HTTP 429) under burst load
failure_modes.mitigation: Explanation requests are serialized through an in-process async queue with configurable concurrency limit (default: 5 concurrent LLM calls). When queue depth exceeds 50, new explanation tasks are deferred and the stub is returned permanently for low-priority matches. Rate limit metrics are exposed via GET /api/v1/health?verbose=true for operational monitoring.

failure_modes.scenario: pgvector ANN search query exceeds 150ms timeout
failure_modes.mitigation: Matching engine wraps each vector query with a 150ms asyncio timeout. On timeout, falls back to keyword-based Jaccard skill matching for ranking (workstyle_score and motivation_score default to 0.5 neutral value). The fallback is logged and the match response includes a flag 'vector_search_degraded: true'. Alert fires if fallback rate exceeds 1% over a 5-minute window.

failure_modes.scenario: PostgreSQL primary connection pool exhausted under high concurrency
failure_modes.mitigation: asyncpg connection pool is configured with max_size=20, min_size=5, command_timeout=30s. Requests that cannot acquire a connection within 5s receive HTTP 503 with Retry-After: 2 header. Pool size is tunable via environment variable DB_POOL_MAX_SIZE without code change. Phase 2 production deployment adds PgBouncer in transaction-pooling mode.

failure_modes.scenario: Weight misconfiguration: weights submitted that do not sum to 1.0 or contain negative values
failure_modes.mitigation: PUT /api/v1/config/weights validates: all wi >= 0.0, |sum(w1..w5) - 1.0| < 0.001. Returns HTTP 400 with error detail listing the invalid weights. Previous weight configuration is preserved unchanged. Unit test in test_weight_config.py verifies rejection of invalid payloads.

failure_modes.scenario: GDPR cascade erasure partial failure (e.g., pgvector DELETE fails mid-transaction)
failure_modes.mitigation: All six deletion operations (profile row, developer_embeddings rows, MatchRecord rows, FeedbackRecord rows, AllocationRecord rows, ExplanationCache rows) are wrapped in a single PostgreSQL SAVEPOINT. On any operation failure, ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT restores all data. HTTP 500 is returned with an erasure_request_id. An ErasureAuditLog row is created with status='failed' for compliance auditing and manual remediation. The developer profile remains intact — no partial deletion.

failure_modes.scenario: Embedding generation failure for a new developer profile (embedding model API error or invalid vector format)
failure_modes.mitigation: Profile is persisted to relational-db immediately with embedding_status='pending'. The embedding_worker marks status='failed' after 3 retry attempts. The matching engine detects 'failed' or 'pending' status and returns a degraded match response using keyword-only skill scoring (vector dimensions default to 0.5). A badge 'behavioral_data_unavailable' is included in the match response to alert the developer that full behavioral matching is not yet active.

failure_modes.scenario: Stale weight configuration read across multiple API replicas after PUT /api/v1/config/weights
failure_modes.mitigation: WeightConfig is always read from the PostgreSQL primary on each match request — no in-process caching. This ensures all replicas see updated weights immediately without cache invalidation logic. The trade-off is one extra DB read per match request (< 5ms). For Phase 2, an optional Redis cache with 60-second TTL and explicit cache-bust on PUT /config/weights can be added.

failure_modes.scenario: Rejection-rate analytics surface misleading statistics with fewer than 10 feedback samples
failure_modes.mitigation: The feedback-analytics-service enforces a minimum sample floor: rejection_ratio is returned as null and min_sample_floor_met is returned as false when sample_count < 10. Manager dashboard renders a 'Insufficient data' badge instead of a percentage. The minimum floor of 10 is configurable via REJECTION_RATE_MIN_SAMPLES environment variable without code change.

failure_modes.scenario: Vector search cold-start degrades match quality for early deployments with fewer than 50 profiles
failure_modes.mitigation: Alembic data migration seeds >= 50 DeveloperProfile rows and >= 20 ProjectProfile rows with pre-computed embeddings as part of the initial schema migration. This ensures the HNSW index has sufficient vectors for meaningful ANN search from day one. The HNSW index requires a minimum of 1 vector to be created; quality improves monotonically as real profiles are added.

failure_modes.scenario: JWT secret rotation or expiry causes all authenticated sessions to be invalidated simultaneously
failure_modes.mitigation: NEURAL_SYNC_JWT_SECRET is loaded from environment variable at startup. Rotation requires a rolling restart of API replicas. Access token TTL=1h minimizes the window of active tokens. In Phase 1, graceful rotation is acceptable (users must re-login after restart). Phase 2 introduces key-id (kid) claim support for zero-downtime secret rotation using multiple valid secrets.
